steps.speech: The rock cycle often starts with Igneous rock, formed when magma cools and solidifies.
Over millions of years, wind and water break down the rock through weathering and erosion.
These fragments compress to form layered Sedimentary rock.
When pushed deep underground, intense heat and pressure transform it.
It becomes Metamorphic rock. If it gets hot enough, it melts back into magma, and the cycle continues!
